Empires & Puzzles Peppermint Review: Is The Gnome Alchemist Worth It?

Peppermint arrived with the 2022 Christmas event, bringing a cauldron of poison and a grumpy attitude. But does this Gnome Alchemist have the formula for success, or is her kit just a bad smell? We break down her tile-scaling mechanics, her underwhelming minions, and why she might be the 'coal' in your summon gate stocking.
Peppermint: The Grumpy Gnome Alchemist – Poisonous Potions and Underwhelming Explosions

Peppermint, the grumpy Gnome Alchemist, joined the Empires & Puzzles roster during the 2022 Santa's Challenge. A 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the Winter family, she was designed as a "balanced" AoE attacker with a mix of direct damage, minions, and damage over time (DoT). Unfortunately, in the high-octane world of modern E&P, "balanced" often translates to "benchwarmer." While her visual design suggests a potent brew of magic, her actual impact on the battlefield often feels more like a light spritz of eau de toilette than a lethal chemical weapon.

Peppermint's art is a delightful departure from the usual "fierce warrior" aesthetic. She looks like she hasn't slept in three weeks and hates every second of being awake. The grumpy expression, the strange insect-like contraptions on her hat, and the glowing censer (which presumably contains the "Putrid Perfume") give her a unique, albeit slightly gross, charm. She embodies the chaotic energy of a workshop elf who’s tired of making toys and has switched to brewing hazardous chemicals. It's a fun design that perfectly captures her lore as a gnome alchemist, even if her in-game performance doesn't quite match her menacing glare.

Let's be blunt: Peppermint's stats are a relic of the past. With a base Power of 851 and stats hovering around 824 Attack and 836 Defense, she is drastically behind the curve. Modern heroes (released in late 2025/2026) are boasting base stats pushing 1300+ in Attack and Defense with nearly double her health pool. Even with a second Limit Break (LB2), her stats barely scrape the 1000 mark, meaning a fully maxed Peppermint today has lower stats than a naked, non-emblemed modern hero. This stat deficit makes her incredibly fragile and her damage output laughable against modern defenses.
"Putrid Perfume" tries to do three things at once and excels at none of them.

250% damage to all enemies is simply too low for an Average speed hero in the current meta. We have Fast heroes hitting harder, and modern Slow/Average heroes are often clearing the board with 450%+ damage.

The Elf Minions inherit 10% HP and 10% Attack. In an era of Mega Minions and minions with 20-30% inheritance, these elves are little more than paper tissue. They die to a stiff breeze and provide negligible protection.

The poison damage scales with Dark shields on the board, capping at 408 over 4 turns. While the mechanic is interesting, the damage cap is pitifully low. 408 damage over 4 turns is roughly 100 damage per turn. Modern DoT heroes deal 500-1000+ damage straight out of the gate without needing board conditions.
Average (10 tiles). This is too slow for the impact she provides.
Base: 250% to 5 enemies = 1250% total damage output.
With modern stats (Attack ~824 vs Defense ~1500), this 250% will likely result in "1 damage" scratches against top-tier tanks.
The 10% HP minion on a hero with ~1500 HP (LB1) provides roughly 150 HP buffer. This is easily cleared by a single slash attack or incidental AoE damage.
Max (requires 8+ Dark shields): 408 damage / 4 turns = 102 damage/turn.
The scaling reward is not worth the effort of saving tiles.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Winter Family bonus boosts the health and attack of minions. Since Peppermint summons minions for everyone, she does benefit from this if you run a full Christmas team (e.g., with Mother North or Krampus). However, a 2-3% boost to already weak minions is lipstick on a pig.
Peppermint suffers from "Old Hero Syndrome"—she has
innate passive skills outside of the family bonus. No mana boost on buff, no healing on ailment, nothing.

This increases the damage of her special skill when Limit Broken. While damage is what she needs, a percentage boost on a low base percentage (250%) yields diminishing returns compared to boosting a high-damage sniper.

Even upon her release in December 2022, Peppermint was met with a lukewarm reception. She was considered "balanced" in a time when players were looking for the next meta-breaker. She never truly had a moment in the spotlight, overshadowed by her family members like Mother North, Krampus, and even Pengi.
The game has sprinted past Peppermint. Her damage is negligible, her minions are irrelevant, and her DoT is cleansable and weak.
There is almost no scenario in competitive play where Peppermint is the optimal choice. She sits firmly in the "Soul Exchange fodder" category for veteran players.
Note: At the time of this review, Peppermint does not have a costume.
This section highlights a major disadvantage: without a costume, she lacks the stat bonus that keeps older heroes like Horghall or Quintus somewhat relevant (or at least survivable).
If you insist on using her (perhaps for the Sorcerer class quests or purely for fun), you need to keep her alive long enough to cast.
Increases her survivability, allowing her to potentially fire her skill more than once. The increased health also slightly boosts her minion HP.
Her damage, already low, will remain underwhelming.
Maximizes the hit-all damage and DoT ticks.
With her outdated stats, she will likely die before charging her Average speed mana.
path is recommended. If she's on your team, she's likely there to provide meat shields (minions) and chip damage, so staying alive is priority #1.
Hitting all enemies is always useful for finishing off weakened teams.
Provides a small buffer of health to the entire team.
Sorcerer class allows a chance to slow mana generation, which can be annoying on a hit-all skill.
Completely left behind by power creep.
Too slow for the low impact of the skill.
Lacks the utility of modern heroes.
Her hit-all animation is reasonably fast, making her okay for auto-farming low-level map stages.
The only place she might see play. At Very Fast speed, the 250% damage and minion summon become "okay" spam, but there are still dozens of better purple options (e.g., Killhare, Grimble).
Note: "Advanced" here implies trying to make a sub-par hero work.
. Peppermint summons a minion for everyone, which fuels Pengi's chain attack. This is her one true niche.
Maximize the Winter Family bonus and give Pengi plenty of ammo.
You need Defense Down (Tiburtus) and Attack Up (Rigard) to make Peppermint's damage feel significant.
Grimble, Ogima, and newer heroes like Topaz or Eloise eat Peppermint's team for breakfast.
Any healer who cleanses makes her DoT useless.
Prevents her from hitting everyone (though she is AoE, taunt usually restricts targeting).
5-star hero, congratulations! Max her out. A 5-star hero is always better than a 3-star. She will carry you through map stages and early raids. However, do
Limit Break her (use Aethers) or give her Master Emblems. Save those precious resources for a more modern hero.
She is 100% Soul Exchange fodder. There is no reason to level her over almost any other purple 5-star project. Even classic heroes with costumes are often better.
The community has never been kind to Peppermint. Even on her release day, the sentiment was harsh:
"There has to be a dud in every portal. Peppermint is the dud."
"This card would be obsolete even in 2020."
"Wow… just pulled this turd… really disappointed."
Another noted her lack of impact:
"In defense, utter rubbish... Peppermint doesn't scare me."
These quotes highlight that she was undertuned from day one, and time has only made it worse.

Peppermint is a hero with a fun design and a grim fate. She suffers from being a "jack of all trades, master of none" in a game that rewards specialists and raw power. Her potions are watered down, her minions are fragile, and her stats belong in a museum. Unless you are building a thematic Christmas defense team for fun or feeding Pengi, Peppermint is best left on the bench—or traded away at the Soul Exchange for someone who packs a real punch.
A stocking stuffer of coal. Only level if you have absolutely no other 5-star Dark heroes.
A comprehensive review of Peppermint, the 5-star Dark hero from the Winter Family. We analyze her stats, special skill, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
